title: Phlegmariurus billardierei
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus billardierei is a species of lycopod in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Phlegmariurus billardierei is a member of the division Lycopodiophyta, class Lycopodiopsida, and order Lycopodiales. This plant is further classified as a genus in the family Lycopodiaceae and is closely related to other species in the same family. As a member of the Lycopodiaceae family, Phlegmariurus billardierei is one of the many species of club mosses.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help in soothing skin irritations and infections. intro: Phlegmariurus billardierei, also known as the Australian forked fork, is a type of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress.
